states.name: OffIdle_GSC_TRUE
states.description: Device is off without an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. No periodic 100 ms timer is considered active for OnTime/OffWaitTime management. GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.

states.name: OffIdle_GSC_FALSE
states.description: Device is off without an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. No periodic 100 ms timer is considered active for OnTime/OffWaitTime management. GlobalSceneControl is FALSE.

states.name: OnIdle_GSC_TRUE
states.description: Device is on without an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. No periodic 100 ms timer is considered active for OnTime/OffWaitTime management. GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.

states.name: OnIdle_GSC_FALSE
states.description: Device is on without an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. No periodic 100 ms timer is considered active for OnTime/OffWaitTime management. GlobalSceneControl is FALSE.

states.name: TimedOn_GSC_TRUE
states.description: Device is on with an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. OnTime > 0 is being decremented every 100 ms until it reaches 0, at which point the device turns off and OffWaitTime is set to 0 and the timer is stopped. GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.

states.name: TimedOn_GSC_FALSE
states.description: Device is on with an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence. OnTime > 0 is being decremented every 100 ms until it reaches 0, at which point the device turns off and OffWaitTime is set to 0 and the timer is stopped. GlobalSceneControl is FALSE.

states.name: DelayedOff_GSC_TRUE
states.description: Device is off with an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence protecting the Off state. OffWaitTime > 0 is being decremented every 100 ms until it reaches 0, at which point the timer is stopped. GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.

states.name: DelayedOff_GSC_FALSE
states.description: Device is off with an active OnWithTimedOff timing sequence protecting the Off state. OffWaitTime > 0 is being decremented every 100 ms until it reaches 0, at which point the timer is stopped. GlobalSceneControl is FALSE.

notes:
The FSM has been normalized to be fully explicit and deterministic:
- **State Expansion**: The `GlobalSceneControl` attribute (defaulting to TRUE) was expanded into state names (e.g., `OffIdle` became `OffIdle_GSC_TRUE` and `OffIdle_GSC_FALSE`) to explicitly represent its impact on state behavior and ensure determinism.
- **Conditional Actions Eliminated**: All `if...then...end if` constructs within action lists were removed. The conditions were extracted and transformed into explicit guards, resulting in multiple, distinct transitions for each original conditional action.
- **Guard Normalization**: Guards were made mutually exclusive and exhaustive for every `(from_state, trigger)` pair. Implicit conditions (e.g., `OnOff == TRUE` when in an 'On' state) were leveraged for guard simplification. Conditions like `(OnTime - 1) == 0` were simplified to `OnTime == 1`.
- **Feature Handling**: Explicit `discard_command()` transitions were added for commands that require `feature(LT) == TRUE` when `feature(LT)` is `FALSE`. This ensures that all `(state, trigger)` pairs have a defined, deterministic behavior, even for unsupported features.
- **Action Normalization**: All comments and pseudo-code were removed from action lists, leaving only atomic, unconditional actions.
- **Initial State**: The initial state is set to `OffIdle_GSC_TRUE`, consistent with the default value of `GlobalSceneControl` being `TRUE`.
